acceptanceSignals: MCP tools/resources cover ordering, verification, compliance review, settlement query/export, and anomaly operations.
Parity tests confirm MCP and HTTP share validation, authz, and error behavior.
High-risk MCP writes are authorization-checked and fully audited.

attempts.latestEventSummary: feature completed: MCP Server Domain Parity in 0 attempts

lastCommit.message: feat: implement MCP Server Domain Parity (attempt 4)

reinforcementResults.name: HTTP Contract Sync Gate
reinforcementResults.summary: The MCP Server Domain Parity feature satisfies the active policy in this workspace: MCP tools/resources cover the required domains, OAuth service-account plus tool-level RBAC is enforced with strict optional bridge-key controls and auditable write authorization, MCP invocation reuses shared HTTP/domain handlers, and OpenAPI plus generated TypeScript artifacts are synchronized with route/spec and error-schema conformance checks wired in CI.

reinforcementResults.name: MCP Domain Parity Gate
reinforcementResults.summary: Pass based on static workspace review: MCP endpoints enforce OAuth service-account authentication with tool-level RBAC, optional bridge keys are guarded by TTL/rotation/registry checks and audit metadata, MCP write invocations are authorization-checked and audited, MCP tools/resources cover ordering/verification/compliance/settlement/anomaly domains, and MCP invocation paths reuse the same handler/domain execution used by HTTP with paired MCP/HTTP parity tests present for validation/error/authz behavior.

reinforcementResults.name: High-Risk Write Audit Discipline
reinforcementResults.summary: The current MCP Server Domain Parity workspace satisfies the active policy: MCP uses OAuth service-account auth with tool-level RBAC, optional bridge keys are constrained by TTL/rotation/registry checks and audited, MCP invokes shared HTTP/domain execution paths (not MCP-only logic), and high-risk MCP writes are authorization-gated and recorded through the unified immutable audit trail with attributable fields (actorId, role, entityId, reason, timestamp) plus correlationId.

reinforcementResults.name: No Legacy Path Rule
reinforcementResults.summary: The current workspace satisfies the MCP Server Domain Parity policy: MCP tools/resources cover ordering, verification, compliance review, settlement query/export, and anomaly operations; MCP auth uses OAuth service-account grants with tool-level RBAC plus strict short-lived bridge-key rotation/registry/audit controls; MCP write paths are authorization-gated and emit audit evidence; and MCP execution routes through the same domain handlers/services used by HTTP paths with parity-focused tests present for validation/authz/error behavior. (Targeted test execution could not be re-run in this read-only sandbox, but the committed implementation and test coverage evidence align with the active policy.)

reinforcementResults.name: Data Retention Enforcement
reinforcementResults.summary: Based on the current workspace code and test artifacts, this feature satisfies the active policy: MCP uses OAuth service-account authentication with tool-level RBAC (plus strictly validated short-lived bridge keys), MCP write authorization is audited, MCP execution reuses shared HTTP/domain handlers, and payroll/order/audit retention-deletion workflows are implemented with schedulable jobs, encryption and access-control test coverage, and audit-query traceability of purge outcomes.

excludedApproaches: A separate MCP-only domain logic implementation.
Unscoped broad MCP credentials without tool-level RBAC.
executionConstraints: Use OAuth service-account + tool-level RBAC as the core MCP authz model.
Support optional short-lived key bridging only under strict rotation and audit controls.
MCP must call the same domain services used by HTTP APIs.
name: MCP Server Domain Parity
rationale: Agent integration is a required first-class surface with strict security parity needs.
summary: Implement secure MCP access over shared domain logic and permission controls.
